# RustyNES v2.0.5 — "Harbor" ("Landfall")

**The iOS re-port onto Timebase.** The fifth release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" mobile-finalization train opens the **iOS finalization window (v2.0.5 → v2.0.8)** — the iOS/iPadOS analogue of the Android v2.0.1 → v2.0.4 window. It brings the frozen v1.9.9 SwiftUI / Metal app onto the **v2.0.0 "Timebase"** core. The cycle-accurate emulation core is **unchanged and byte-identical to v2.0.4** — **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%)**, nestest 0-diff, the deterministic `#![no_std]` chip stack untouched — so **no accuracy, save-state, or determinism number moves in this release**. This is a host / iOS-only cut.

No store submission happens here (that is the future v2.1.0 joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch); v2.0.5 establishes the green iOS build path on the v2.0.0 core and re-ports the first host-side behaviour the timebase break requires.

---

## The core is unchanged (141/141)

Nothing in the emulation core changed. The 2-cycle-ALE PPU fetch model promoted to default in v2.0.3 (ADR 0030) is the substrate here too; AccuracyCoin holds **141/141 (100.00%, RAM-authoritative)**, nestest is 0-diff, and the 60-ROM commercial byte-identity oracle is unmoved. If you only care about emulation accuracy, v2.0.5 is v2.0.4 with a bumped version number.

## Host-side iOS re-port (what actually landed)

- **The pre-Timebase movie warning, surfaced + localized on iOS.** The v2.0.0
  "Timebase" rewrite bumped the `.rns`/`.rnm` save-state + movie epoch (ADR 0028):
  a pre-v2.0.0 `.rnm` still **replays its input stream** on the new core, but exact
  framebuffer / audio reproduction is no longer guaranteed across the timebase
  change. The v2.0.3 bridge began queuing a machine-readable `HostWarning` code for
  this so hosts can localize it; **v2.0.5 surfaces it on iOS** — a new, non-blocking
  notice (distinct from the error alert) presented after a movie load, drained via
  `EmulatorCore.drainWarnings()` → `NesController.drainWarningCodes()`, localized
  **EN + ES** with wording byte-identical to the Android v2.0.4 string (shared ES
  copy). Loading a pre-Timebase movie now tells the user exactly what to expect
  instead of silently diverging.
- **UniFFI-Swift binding surface re-confirmed against the v2.0.0 bridge.** The
  generated Swift surface (`drainWarningCodes() -> [HostWarning]`,
  `HostWarning.preTimebaseMovie`, `moviePlay(bytes:)`) was regenerated from the
  v2.0.0 `rustynes-mobile` and confirmed to match the host code — the iOS analogue
  of the wasm-bindgen-pin discipline that keeps the Rust library and the generated
  bindings in lockstep.
- **Version realignment.** Workspace `2.0.4 → 2.0.5` (version-only `Cargo.lock`
  cascade). The iOS `MARKETING_VERSION` is realigned from the frozen v1.9.x
  checked-in default (`1.9.1`) to `2.0.5`, back in step with the workspace version;
  the build number stays CI-injected by the fastlane `beta` lane.

## Verification (host)

- **Core unchanged / byte-identical to v2.0.4** — AccuracyCoin **141/141**, nestest
  0-diff (not re-run for this host-only cut; the chip stack is untouched).
- `cargo check --workspace` green after the version bump + `Cargo.lock` regen
  (version-only lock diff, 18 crates); `cargo fmt --all --check` clean.
- **UniFFI-Swift emit host-verified:** the Swift bindings generated from a host
  build of `rustynes-mobile` contain `drainWarningCodes`, `HostWarning`
  (`case preTimebaseMovie`), and `moviePlay(bytes:)` — the exact surface the new
  Swift code references.
- `Localizable.xcstrings` remains valid JSON (EN source + the added ES entry).
- markdownlint (CI-pinned) clean on the new / edited docs.

## Maintainer / on-device closeout (deferred to v2.0.9 / the TestFlight upload)

These are inherently macOS / on-device / maintainer steps and are **not** done by
this release (there is no Xcode / device in the build environment):

- Regenerate the `RustyNESFFI.xcframework` on macOS (`scripts/build-ios-xcframework.sh`)
  and archive the app with **Xcode 26 / the iOS 26 SDK** (the App Store submission
  floor lands as a hard gate at v2.0.7).
- On-device **save-state migration** from a v1.9.x install and the **AccuracyCoin /
  SMB / Zelda determinism smoke** on Apple silicon.
- A physical-device **TestFlight boot smoke** (iPhone 14+ / iPad); confirm the
  pre-Timebase-movie notice renders correctly under both locales.

## What's next — the rest of "Harbor"

- **v2.0.6** — iOS feature-parity finalization against the v2.0.0 behaviour.
- **v2.0.7** — iOS polish + performance + the **iOS 26 SDK / Xcode 26** App Store
  submission-floor readiness + the privacy-manifest / required-reason-API audit.
- **v2.0.8** — the iOS release candidate (App Store Connect listing staged,
  TestFlight external beta, App-Review self-audit).
- **v2.0.9** — joint on-device readiness verification for both apps.
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid launch**.

Full detail: `CHANGELOG.md` `[2.0.5]` · `docs/STATUS.md` (single source of truth) · `docs/ios-v2.0.5-readiness.md` · `to-dos/plans/v2.0.5-v2.0.8-ios-finalization-plan.md`.
